vulnerabilities.name: Secret: Generic API Key
vulnerabilities.message: Hardcoded API key found in configuration file
vulnerabilities.description: Gitleaks detected a hardcoded API key. Rule: generic-api-key. Exposing secrets in source code allows attackers to authenticate as the application and access protected resources.
vulnerabilities.solution: Remove the secret from source code immediately. Rotate the exposed key. Use environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g. HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) to inject secrets at runtime.
vulnerabilities.ai_analysis:
This hardcoded API key gives anyone with repository access full authentication credentials. An attacker could impersonate the application, exfiltrate data, or incur costs.

Step 1: Revoke the exposed key immediately in the provider's console.
Step 2: Replace with os.getenv('API_KEY') and set via environment variable.
Step 3: Add pre-commit hooks with gitleaks to prevent future leaks.

vulnerabilities.name: Secret: JWT Token
vulnerabilities.message: JWT secret key hardcoded in application source
vulnerabilities.description: A JWT signing secret was found hardcoded in the Express.js configuration. An attacker who obtains this value can forge valid authentication tokens for any user.
vulnerabilities.solution: Generate a strong random secret (min 256 bits) and store it in an environment variable. Never hardcode cryptographic secrets.

vulnerabilities.name: SQL Injection via string concatenation
vulnerabilities.message: User input directly concatenated into SQL query without sanitisation
vulnerabilities.description: Semgrep detected a SQL injection vulnerability. The value of req.query.id is concatenated directly into a SQL string passed to db.query(). An attacker can manipulate this parameter to execute arbitrary SQL.
vulnerabilities.solution: Use parameterised queries or prepared statements. Replace string concatenation with query parameters: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [req.query.id])

vulnerabilities.name: Reflected XSS via res.send
vulnerabilities.message: Unsanitised user input reflected in HTTP response
vulnerabilities.description: User-supplied search term is rendered directly into the response HTML without HTML-encoding. An attacker can craft a URL with a malicious script payload that executes in the victim's browser.
vulnerabilities.solution: HTML-encode all user input before rendering. Use a template engine that auto-escapes output, or explicitly call a sanitisation function such as DOMPurify.

vulnerabilities.name: lodash — CVE-2021-23337
vulnerabilities.description: lodash versions before 4.17.21 are vulnerable to command injection via the template function. An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary code on the server.
vulnerabilities.solution: Upgrade lodash to version 4.17.21 or later: npm install lodash@latest

vulnerabilities.name: express — CVE-2024-43796
vulnerabilities.description: Express.js versions before 4.20.0 are vulnerable to an open redirect when the res.redirect() function is passed unsanitised user input.
vulnerabilities.solution: Upgrade express to version 4.20.0 or later: npm install express@latest

vulnerabilities.name: Container running as root
vulnerabilities.message: Dockerfile does not set a non-root USER directive
vulnerabilities.description: KICS detected that the container runs as root by default. If the container is compromised, an attacker has full root access to the container's filesystem.
vulnerabilities.solution: Add a USER directive to the Dockerfile to run the process as an unprivileged user: RUN addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app\nUSER app

vulnerabilities.name: HTTPS not enforced in Kubernetes Ingress
vulnerabilities.message: Ingress resource does not redirect HTTP to HTTPS
vulnerabilities.description: The Kubernetes Ingress resource does not configure the nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect annotation. Traffic may be served over unencrypted HTTP.
vulnerabilities.solution: Add the annotation nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true" to enforce HTTPS.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
vulnerabilities.message: Login endpoint has no brute-force protection
vulnerabilities.description: The /api/user/login endpoint has no rate-limiting middleware. An attacker can make unlimited login attempts to brute-force user credentials.
vulnerabilities.solution: Add rate-limiting middleware (e.g. express-rate-limit) to authentication endpoints. Implement account lockout after N failed attempts.

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure cookie: missing Secure flag
vulnerabilities.message: Session cookie created without the Secure attribute
vulnerabilities.description: The session cookie is set without the Secure flag, meaning it will be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections. An attacker performing a man-in-the-middle attack can steal the session token.
vulnerabilities.solution: Set cookie options: { secure: true, httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'strict' }

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure cookie: missing HttpOnly flag
vulnerabilities.message: Session cookie accessible to JavaScript (missing HttpOnly)
vulnerabilities.description: The session cookie can be read by JavaScript running on the page. An XSS vulnerability elsewhere could allow an attacker to steal session tokens via document.cookie.
vulnerabilities.solution: Add httpOnly: true to all session and authentication cookie options.

vulnerabilities.name: jsonwebtoken — CVE-2022-23529
vulnerabilities.message: Remote code execution vulnerability in jsonwebtoken < 9.0.0
vulnerabilities.description: jsonwebtoken versions before 9.0.0 are vulnerable to remote code execution when an attacker controls the secretOrPublicKey option passed to jwt.verify().
vulnerabilities.solution: Upgrade jsonwebtoken to version 9.0.0 or later: npm install jsonwebtoken@latest
